titleStmt.title: SII 25.176: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 176. (A.R. No. 176 of 1908.) TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.
This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pūmaruviya-tiśaimugattōn, etc., of the king.
The details of date of the record viz., 5th year, Kuṁbha ba 9, Friday, Mūla corresponds to 18th February, 1183 A.D.

It states that a kūṟṟ-ōlai was executed by the ūravar to one Māttuḻāṉ Chāmuṇḍadēvaṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ of Māttūr in Kumiḻi-nāḍu in Āmūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam and to his marumagaḷ śrī Pugaḷūrdēvaṉ Peṟṟāḻvi for the lands of araiyē-nālu-mā enjoyed by them. Of these 14 mā of lands, after deducting the 7 mā of lands belonging to śrī Pugaḷūrdēvaṉ Peṟṟāḻvi, the remaining 7 mā of lands were got exchanged (parivattaṉai) from Vimiśiram-uḍaiyāṉ Rājarājadēvaṉ. In this after excluding the lands of kāṇi-araikkāṇi-mundirigai given earlier to the temple of Tāmattīśuram-uḍaiyār, the balance of āru-mā-arai-mundirigai were endowed to provide for the food-offerings to the deity Āḷuḍaiya-piḷḷaiyār in the temple of Śirāḷīśuram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, the gift being entrusted to the Śrīmāhēśvaras; to the goddess Āḷuḍaiya-Nāchchiyār in the tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai of the temple of Śīrāḷīśuram-uḍaiyār, to the god Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyār and his Nāchchiyār and Vināyakap-piḷḷaiyār (all the three) being worshipped by the donor's father, to the god Adirpaḍa-Āḍavallār in the temple of Siddhīśuram at Tirunaṟaiyūr in the nāḍu (of the same name) for the coating, processional ceremony and food-offerings to be made on the day of Vaikāśi festival and to the deity Tirumāḷigaip-piḷḷaiyār in the temple of Tāmattīśuram-uḍaiyār for the purpose of food-offerings. Of the ēḻu-mā-kāṇi-araikkāṇi kīḻ-mukkālē mūṉṟu-māk-kāṇi obtained in exchange from Tiruvēgambam-uḍaiyāṉ Ponmēnda-perumāḷ, the provision of food offerings was made to the deity Tirumāḷigai-piḷḷaiyār by raising the crops after reclamation to the extent of kāṇi-araikkāṇi-kiḻ-mukkālē-mūnṟu-māk-kāṇi lands.

The royal order (tirumugam) refers to the gift of these lands as neyttōṟpaṭṭi-iṟaiyili for the donor's father. A portion of the iṟaiyili gift should be enjoyed as capital by the person who has received the donor share (ōpādik-kāṇi). It is stipulated that he should pay the taxes annually for the share of land which had been declared as nettōṟpaṭṭi (i.e., the lands endowed to the heirs of the persons who lost their lives in fighting), failing which the donor's ōpādi-kāṇi should be distributed.

This inscription (No. 176) of Vīrarājēndra, identified with Kulōttuṅga III is interesting in more than respect. It makes a particular reference to the gift lands made by Māttuḻān Chāmuṇḍadēvan Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ for his father (pitā) as neyttōṟ-paṭṭi-iṟaiyili. It is also referred to as nettōṟpaṭṭi. The lands which were in enjoyment of the donor were given. It is a taxfree gift of the lands given to the heirs of persons who lost their lives in fighting or in the course of services rendered to the king or to his representative.

The term neyttōṟpaṭṭi is the same as its variant udirappaṭṭi occurring in a number of Tamil records. The expression ney in Tamil means ‘blood’. The Kannaḍa nettar and the Telugu netturu or netra or rakta are all similar expressions which should have been derived from a common Dravidian root. The word neyttōṟ should have been corrupted into nettōṟ meaning ‘blood’ and the gift given as neyttōṟpaṭṭi can be understood as the grant made to the descendants of the deceased who must have shed their blood in the wars.
The synonymous expressions netturuviddha and nettravaṭṭi figures, in the Telugu records of 12th century A.D. (S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 172; S.I.I., Vol. V. No. 1014).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . pūmaruviya ticaimukatton paṭaitta perum puvi viḷaṅkat temmaruviya pakantuḷava tiruneṭumālivaneṉṉa nirāḻi puṭaicūḻntavarai Āḻi neṭumpuṟattil Orāḻi taniṉaṭātta Utaiyakulattavatarittu karuṅkaliyiṉveyil virikkum katirveṇkuṭai nilāveṟikkum varuṅkatiyiriṉ veyil virikkum maṇimakuṭam puṉaintaruḷi cempoṉ virasiṅṅātaṉattu tiripuvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruṉtaruḷiya kopparakecaripaṟmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ si virarājendhratevaṟku yāṇṭu Aiñcāvatu kumpanāyaṟṟu Aparapa(k)kṣattu navamiyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa mulattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu Āmūrkoṭṭa
ttu kumiḻināṭṭu māttūr māttuḻāṉ cāmuṇṭatevaṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāneṉku(l)lottuṅka coḻavaḷa nāṭṭu tirunaṟaiyūr nāṭṭu māttūril Eṉṉutāyum nāṉ vilai koṇṭuṭaiyeṉāyum nān vicattaṭai parivarttanai paṇṇi Uṭaiyeṉāyum ṉān Anupovittu varukiṟa ṉilattukku parivarttaṉait tiṭṭukkaḷinpaṭiyum yiṉakkum nāṉkuṭuttuṭaiyaāḷāka En marumakaḷ śrī pukalūrtevaṉ peṟṟāḻivikkum Oru kūṟṟolaiyāka Eḻuti Ūravar Eḻuttiṭṭu nāṅkaḷ Aṉupovittu varukiṟa nilaṅkaḷil yilantaikkāleṉṟu per kūvappaṭṭa ṉilattukkuk kiḻpārkkellai Uṭciṟuvāykkālukku meṟkum melpālkellai Āvaṇa
ttu nattattukkum Ūrkkaṇakkattivicattukkuk kiḻakkum teṉpākkellai kuḷattukku vaṭakkum vaṭakiḻakkum vaṭapārkellai tāmatticiram muṭaiya nāyanār koyil Ellaikkum vāykkālukkum tekkum Āka yicainta perunāṉ kellaiyuḷ naṭuvupaṭṭanilam Araiye nālumāvil kiḻkkaṭaiya yiṉta cipukalūrtevaṉ peṟṟāḻviṉilam Eḻumānikki ninṟanilam Eḻumāvil nāṅkaḷ kūṟṟolaiceta pinpu vimicirammuṭaiyāṉ Irā[ja*]rāja tevanuṭaṉ vicattaṭaippirivattaṉaiyākak kūṭṭamuṭaiyāṉenṟu perkūvappaṭṭa nilattuk kuṭuttu Innilattukku talaimāṟu Ivilaṉtaikkālil Ivan koṇṭuṭaiya nilamuṉṟu māvum talaimāṟu kaikkoṇṭu Itan meṟkūṟṟolaippaṭiyā varuki
ṟa nilam nālumāvum Āka Eṉṉutāṉa nilam Eḻumāvum tāmatticuramuṭaiyārkku koyil Uṭaṉcera munṉāḷil nirvāttu kuṭutta nilam kāṇi Araikkāṇimuntirikai nikki nilam Aṟumāvarai muntirikai nikki nilam Aṟumāvarai muntirikai Itil cirāḷi curamuṭaiyanāyanār koyil Āḷuṭaiya piḷḷaiyārkku nāḷonṟukku Oru potaikku Irunāḻi Ariciyum kaṟiyamutuḷḷiṭṭa viñcanaṅkaḷuṅ koṇṭamutu ceyvittu śrīmāheśvaraṟku prasādamāka kuṭukka nirvāttu kuṭutta meṟkaṭaiya nilam Orumāvum Itaṉ kiḻakkiṟ koyil cirāḷicuramuṭaiyār tiruppaḷḷi yaṟai Āḷuṭaiya nācciyārkku Amutu ceytaruḷa nā
ḷoṉṟukku Irunāḻi Ariciyum kaṟiyamutu Uḷḷiṭṭa viñcanamum celutta kaṭavarkaḷāka nirvārttuk kuṭutta nilam Orumāvu mitan kiḻakku Eṉ pitā pūcitta nāyanār tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyārkkum nācciyārkkum vināyakappiḷḷaiyārkkum tiruvamutukku nirvārttu kuṭutta nilam morumāvu mitaṉ kiḻakku yin nāṭṭut tirunaṟaiyūr citticcurattu Atirpaṭavāṭavallārkku tiruvaikācit tirunāḷaikku tiruccāntu cātti Eḻuntaruḷukaikkum veṇṭum viñcanaṅkaḷukkum Atirpaṭa Āṭavallār cikattattu nirvārttu kuṭutta nilam mummāvaraimuntiri
kaiyum Iṉṉa mittāmatticuramuṭaiyār koyil tirumāḷikaippiḷḷaiyārkku tiruvamutu ceytaruḷa tiruvekampamuṭaiyān ponmenta perumāḷuṭaṉ nāṅkaḷ kūṟṟolai ceytapiṉpu Ivaruṭaṉ vicattaṭai parivarttaṉai Āka Akarattukkuḷattukku kiḻ karaintatu viṭṭu yitukku talaimāṟu nānkaikoṇṭa tāmatticuramuṭaiyār koyilukku melai tirunaṉtavanattukku meṟku Ūrkaṇakku civitattukku vaṭakku meṟpaṭi nilattukku kiḻakku vāykkālukku teṟkku Āka yivvicainta perunāṉkellai Uḷ naṭuvupaṭṭa nattaṅkuḻi nāṟpattu munṟemunṟumāvināl nilam kāṇi Araikkā
ṇikkiḻ mukkāle munṟumākkāṇiyum tiruttivittu nirvārttukkuṭutta yinṉilaṅkoṇṭu yittāmatticcura muṭaiyār koyil tirumāḷikaip piḷḷaiyārkku Amutu celuttakkaṭavārkaḷākavum Āka yinṉilam Eḻumākkāṇi Araikkāṇikiḻ mukkāle muṉṟumākkāṇiyum kaikkoṇṭu yippaṭikaḷ celuttakkaṭavarkaḷākavum yinnilaṅkaḷ Eṉ pitāvukku neyttoṟpaṭṭi yiṟaiyiliyāka prasādañ ceytaruḷina tirumukappaṭi nāṉ Anupavittu varukiṟa nilattukkuṭalāka Aṉupavikka kaṭavarkaḷākavum Iṉnettoṟpaṭṭiyiṟaiyili ciṟitu niṟka ciṟitu māṟuttākil niṉṟa yiṟaiyiliyile Innilamuṭalāvutākavum kaiyyaṟa māṟuttākil Eṉ
opātikkāṇi yaṉupavikku mivaṉevariyiṟuttu Ivviṟaiyili nilainiṟuttik kuṭukka kaṭavanākavum Ippaṭi ceyitavanākil Eṉnopāti kāṇi Ittevarkaḷe kūṟiṭṭuk koḷḷak kaṭavarkaḷākavum Ippaṭikkuṭalāk Enkāṇi yiṟṟaikku piṉpu yum neṟiyum tānamum cellakkaṭavatallātutākavum Ippaṭi sammatittu nirvārttuk kuṭutteṉ Immātuḻān cāmuṇṭa tevan tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāneṉ . Ippaṭikku Ivai māttuḻān cāmuṇṭatevan tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyān Eḻuttu śrīmāhe stops here.
Digital edition of SII 25.176 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
